verdict: High. Forest Ranger scores 42 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

key_tasks.title: Task Analysis
key_tasks.body: task analysis, report writing, problem framing, citation extraction

reasoning_steps.title: Task Analysis
reasoning_steps.body: Routine_cognitive tasks are heavily automated, leaving room for human judgment and problem-solving.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Environment
reasoning_steps.body: The lack of licensure requirements means that Forest Rangers can operate with minimal regulatory oversight.

action_plan.title: Take a course in environmental science or conservation biology
action_plan.body: Gain knowledge on ecosystem management and conservation practices

action_plan.title: Volunteer for conservation projects
action_plan.body: Gain practical experience in forest management and conservation

follow_up_suggestions.title: Research the impact of AI on forestry and conservation efforts
follow_up_suggestions.body: Explore how AI can be used to support forest management and conservation

follow_up_suggestions.title: Network with professionals in the field
follow_up_suggestions.body: Learn about best practices and new developments in forestry and conservation

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.

recent_developments.headline: OECD: about 60% of jobs in advanced economies are exposed to AI
recent_developments.impact: Exposure does not mean replacement — most exposed roles are expected to be transformed rather than eliminated.

recent_developments.headline: Labor market shifts toward AI-complementary skills
recent_developments.impact: Postings increasingly ask for judgment, verification, and direction of AI tools — not just task execution.
